artifacts.content:
From: Marcus DeShawn Ellison <marcus.ellison@gmail.com>
To: Local 399 Grievance Committee <grievances@local399.org>
Date: 2026-02-18 09:42
Subject: Clarification for Arbitration Packet – Ellison, Marcus (FM-4279)

Good morning,

Per our call yesterday, I’m sending additional background you asked for so you can finalize the arbitration packet regarding the County’s recommendation to terminate me from the Cook County Department of Facilities Management.

My current classification is Building Maintenance Engineer II, and my County position ID is FM-4279, assigned to the George N. Leighton Criminal Courthouse. I’ve been in that role since March 2019.

You mentioned you needed more detail on prior discipline, especially anything tied to preventive maintenance. In late July 2025, I agreed to pick up extra overtime at the courthouse to cover another engineer’s vacation. I was behind on the $650 monthly child support payments ordered by the Cook County Circuit Court for my kids, Jalen and Ariana, and I was trying to catch up. By the time the 5th floor air handling unit PM was due on July 29, 2025, I was exhausted from working 12‑hour shifts. I skipped parts of the checklist and rushed the CMMS entry, which later turned out to be incomplete. I understand that incident is being used as part of the progressive discipline history.

I’m not trying to excuse anything, just explain the circumstances so the record reflects what was going on. If you need supporting documents (overtime slips, PM logs, or the child support order), I can scan and email them.

You can reach me at this email or on my cell at 773‑555‑0198.

Thank you for your help on this.

Sincerely,

Marcus D. Ellison
4832 W Cermak Rd, Apt 3B
Cicero, IL 60804

metadata.subject: Clarification for Arbitration Packet – Ellison, Marcus (FM-4279)

artifacts.content:
From: Marcus Ellison <marcus.ellison@cookcountyil.gov>
To: HR Records <hr.records@cookcountyil.gov>
Cc: Union Steward <cseu-local73@unionmail.org>
Date: 2025-09-09 14:18
Subject: Clarification on Job Title and Position Number

Hi Denise,

I’m filling out some disability insurance forms and they’re asking for my “official position ID/number” and department info. I want to make sure I’m using the same thing that’s on file in PeopleSoft.

My badge still shows:

- Classification: Building Maintenance Engineer II
- Department: Facilities Management – Courts & Detention
- Work Location: George N. Leighton Criminal Courthouse / JTDC relief pool
- Position Number printed on old assignment sheet: FM-4213 (not sure if that changed when I started floating more to Juvenile Temporary Detention Center).

Can you confirm whether FM-4213 is still the correct position identifier for me, or if it was updated when they shifted part of my FTE to cover the courthouse PM backlog last year? I don’t want to put the wrong position code on the insurance and union grievance paperwork.

Part of why I’m double‑checking is because some of my discipline notices list slightly different info. For example, the February 11, 2025 write‑up for when I was 22 minutes late to my JTDC shift (after meeting attorney Michael Alvarez on W. Washington to sign the Chapter 13 bankruptcy papers) just says “BME II – FM” with no position number at all.

If there’s a more current position ID (or if I got moved to something like FM-4310 without realizing it), please let me know what HR has in the system.

Thanks,
Marcus
Building Maintenance Engineer II
Cook County Department of Facilities Management

metadata.subject: Clarification on Job Title and Position Number

artifacts.content:
Title: Update Position Code in CMMS / HR Systems
Date: 2025-08-07 09:30–10:30
Location: Teams – Facilities Management Admin
Attendees: Marcus Ellison; Denise Harper (HR); Ron Kaczmarek (FM Supervisor); IT – CMMS Support

Description:

Agenda:
1. Confirm current classification and position code for Marcus Ellison following schedule changes.
2. Align HR position number with CMMS user profile and courthouse assignment sheets.
3. Review impact of recent performance and attendance issues on role designation.

Notes:
- Ron says Marcus is still coded as Building Maintenance Engineer II under Facilities Management – Courts & Detention.
- Denise believes the original position number was FM-3982 when Marcus first came to Leighton in March 2019, but that he was later moved to a pooled position, possibly FM-4391, when they started using him as relief coverage at the Juvenile Temporary Detention Center.
- There is confusion because some discipline documents list “FM-4008” as the position ID, while the CMMS export for preventive maintenance rounds still shows “FM-3982 – BME II – Courts.”
- Marcus explains that in early August 2025 he started missing steps on his PM rounds, especially the 5th floor AHU checklist, due to severe anxiety and insomnia tied to a new depression diagnosis from Dr. Patel at Lawndale Christian Health Center and a medication adjustment on August 3, 2025 that left him groggy during his 6:00 a.m. shifts.
- Action item: Denise will confirm the correct active position code in PeopleSoft and send an email with the final position ID to Marcus and Ron by 8/10/25 so all documentation matches going forward.

metadata.title: Update Position Code in CMMS / HR Systems

artifacts.content:
From: HR Service Desk <hrservicedesk@cookcountyil.gov>
To: Marcus Ellison <marcus.ellison@cookcountyil.gov>
Date: 2025-02-24 11:02
Subject: RE: Request for Position ID for External Forms

Mr. Ellison,

Per your request, this email summarizes the employee and department information currently reflected in the HRIS system for use on your external documentation. Please note that this is not an employment verification letter and should not be shared with third parties as official confirmation of employment.

Employee Name: Ellison, Marcus D.
Job Classification: Building Maintenance Engineer II
Bargaining Unit: SEIU Local 73
Department: Facilities Management – Courts & Detention
Primary Worksite: George N. Leighton Criminal Courthouse (with relief coverage at Juvenile Temporary Detention Center as assigned)

Internal HR Position ID: FM-4335

This internal position ID is the number you should list on any County forms that request your “position id” or “position number.” Some legacy documents may still reference your prior position code FM-3982, which was closed when your assignment was restructured. For avoidance of confusion, HR considers FM-4335 to be your active position identifier as of 01/01/2025.

You mentioned in your earlier email that you needed this for paperwork related to medical and financial issues. Please remember that any explanation regarding your late arrivals on March 4 and March 19, 2025 (12 and 17 minutes late) and the side effects from starting sertraline and trazodone prescribed by Dr. Helen Cho at Rush is considered confidential medical information and should not be detailed on HR forms.

If you need a formal verification letter, please submit a separate request through the Employee Self-Service portal.

Sincerely,

Angela Ruiz
HR Service Desk Analyst
Cook County Department of Human Resources

metadata.subject: RE: Request for Position ID for External Forms

artifacts.content:
Title: Union Prep – Review Position Code & Discipline History
Date: 2025-11-03 15:00–16:15
Location: CSEU Local 73 Office – W. Jackson Blvd, Conference Room B
Attendees: Marcus Ellison; Tony Ramirez (Union Steward)

Description:

Purpose: Prepare for upcoming grievance/arbitration related to recommended termination and clarify what position number and department info should appear on filings.

Agenda:
1. Go over Marcus’s current job classification and position ID.
2. Match position number on disciplinary notices with HR records.
3. Discuss context for 2024–2025 discipline.

Talking points captured in notes:
- Tony advises Marcus to list his current classification as “Building Maintenance Engineer II – Facilities Management (Courts & Detention)” and to use the position code FM-4335 that HR emailed him earlier this year, instead of the old FM-3982 that still shows up on some PM logs.
- There is a discrepancy where one counseling memo from Juvenile Temporary Detention Center lists “BME II – FM-4213,” which Tony thinks was a clerical error.
- Marcus explains again why he was 22 minutes late on February 11, 2025 for his JTDC shift: he had to meet attorney Michael Alvarez on W. Washington to sign Chapter 13 bankruptcy paperwork related to about $38,000 in credit card and medical debt from Ariana’s 2023 asthma hospitalizations at Lurie Children’s, and he didn’t call in because he was embarrassed and trying to keep the bankruptcy private from his supervisor.
- Tony stresses consistency: all grievance forms should use the same position ID (FM-4335) and department name so the County can’t argue a technicality.
- Action: Marcus will bring copies of all write‑ups and any documents that show conflicting position numbers to next week’s prep session.

metadata.title: Union Prep – Review Position Code & Discipline History
metadata.location: CSEU Local 73 Office – W. Jackson Blvd, Conference Room B

artifacts.content:
From: Ron Kaczmarek <ron.kaczmarek@cookcountyil.gov>
To: Scheduling – FM Courts <fm-courts-scheduling@cookcountyil.gov>
Cc: Marcus Ellison <marcus.ellison@cookcountyil.gov>; HR Records <hr.records@cookcountyil.gov>
Date: 2025-08-05 07:41
Subject: Position Code for Ellison on New Leighton Schedule

Team,

I’m updating the Leighton/JTDC rotation chart and need to clean up the position information we’re using for Marcus Ellison. The spreadsheet I inherited still lists him under “BME II – FM-4008,” but HR’s last report I saw in June showed his active position as FM-4335 in the Courts & Detention cost center.

For the next schedule cycle (starting 08/18/25), please list Marcus as:

Name: Ellison, Marcus
Classification: Building Maintenance Engineer II
Department: Facilities Management – Courts & Detention
Position Code (for scheduling purposes only): FM-4008 (legacy code) / mapped internally to FM-4335 in HRIS.

I realize this dual coding is confusing, but IT hasn’t finished the interface work between CMMS and PeopleSoft. Until that’s done, the timekeeping export still references FM-4008. HR has assured me that using the legacy code on the schedule won’t affect his pay or seniority.

FYI, Marcus has mentioned ongoing issues with anxiety and insomnia after a recent medication change by Dr. Patel at Lawndale Christian Health Center. That’s part of why his PM documentation fell behind in early August. This doesn’t change his position number, but it may come up again in the context of his performance reviews and the pending discipline.

Please confirm once the schedule template is updated so we’re consistent across the board.

Thanks,
Ron
Building Maintenance Supervisor
George N. Leighton Criminal Courthouse

metadata.subject: Position Code for Ellison on New Leighton Schedule

Marcus,

Per your request and in preparation for the Step 2 grievance meeting, we pulled your personnel file and historical job data. Please review the information below and note any discrepancies so we can correct them before materials are submitted to Labor Relations.

Employee Name: Ellison, Marcus D.
Employee ID: 006381
Department: Department of Facilities Management – Operations
Division: Courts & Health Facilities
Primary Worksite: George N. Leighton Criminal Courthouse

The system shows the following position identifiers over your County service:

• 2021-07-01 to 2023-12-31 – Building Maintenance Engineer II (Courts), Position ID: FM-4021
• 2024-01-01 to Present – Building Maintenance Engineer II (Rotating Sites), Position ID: FM-4392

Note that the incident on September 18, 2025 in the B-2 mechanical room, where you reported being on the phone with your ex-partner about a possible change to the custody order after her husband’s arrest in Cicero and then rushing through and skipping the 5th floor AHU PM checklist, is logged under the FM-4392 position. Likewise, the August 21, 2025 vehicle misuse at Stroger is tied to the same position record.

If any of the dates or position IDs above are incorrect, reply with corrections by 4:00 PM Wednesday so we can update the HRIS before the arbitration packet is finalized.

Regards,

Angela Ruiz
HR Records Specialist

artifacts.content:
Title: Pre-Arbitration Prep – Review Position History
Date: 2026-02-05 14:30–16:00
Location: Teams Meeting / Home – 4832 W Cermak Rd, Apt 3B
Attendees: Marcus DeShawn Ellison; DFM Labor Liaison (James Patel); AFSCME Steward (R. Coleman)

Description:

Agenda:
1. Confirm employment timeline and position identifiers on record.
2. Walk through major discipline tied to each position.
3. Prep Marcus for likely questions on September 18, 2025 and August 21, 2025 incidents.

Notes to self:
- Double-check that they have me currently listed as Building Maintenance Engineer II under the rotating courts/hospitals assignment. James said HR has my active position ID as FM-4410 in the HRIS, but older paperwork from when I first moved off Leighton might still show FM-4021, so I need to flag that.
- Be ready to explain how the repeated late arrivals on February 6, 14, and 28, 2025 were tied to Jalen’s emergency counseling intake at the Austin Family Mental Health Center after the self-harm incident his school social worker, Ms. Ramirez, reported. That’s part of why my schedule looked so bad on paper.
- For the September 18, 2025 mechanical room issue, be honest about being on the phone with Danielle about the custody order and Caleb’s arrest, and how that led me to rush and skip the 5th floor AHU PM checklist and not log it in the CMMS.

artifacts.content:
From: James Patel <james.patel@cookcountyil.gov>
To: Marcus Ellison <marcus.ellison@cookcountyil.gov>
Date: 2026-01-28 3:41 PM
Subject: Grievance Form – Need Exact Position ID

Marcus,

I’m reviewing the AFSCME grievance form you dropped off this morning regarding the recommended termination after the January 29 safety incident. Section 2 requires your CURRENT position ID, which needs to match what HR has in the system.

I pulled what I could from the HRIS:

Employee: Ellison, Marcus D.
Classification: Building Maintenance Engineer II
Bargaining Unit: AFSCME Council 31
Department: Facilities Management – Courts & Health
Work Location (Primary): George N. Leighton Criminal Courthouse

The HRIS screen I can see shows an internal code of "BME2-CourtOps" and a numeric job code 7312. For the grievance, however, we need the alphanumeric position identifier (the one that starts with "FM-"). HR has a few on file for you over the years: FM-3184 and FM-4021 are both listed as historical, and there’s also FM-4430 tied to your temporary rotation at Stroger around August 21, 2025 when you took the county vehicle to your therapist’s office on Harrison and missed radio calls.

Can you check your last performance evaluation or any recent assignment letter to confirm which FM number is active right now? If you can email me a scan or just reply with the ID by tomorrow at noon, I’ll plug it into the form before submitting to Labor Relations.

Thanks,

James
Labor Liaison
Cook County Department of Facilities Management

metadata.subject: Grievance Form – Need Exact Position ID

artifacts.content:
From: AFSCME Council 31 Intake <intake@afscmec31.org>
To: Marcus Ellison <marcus.ellison@cookcountyil.gov>
Date: 2026-02-06 10:09 AM
Subject: Incomplete Grievance Intake – Missing Position ID

Dear Marcus,

We received your online grievance intake form regarding the proposed discharge from the Cook County Department of Facilities Management. Thank you for submitting the documentation about the January 29, 2026 chemical spill and the prior discipline (including the September 18, 2025 AHU checklist issue and the February 2025 late arrivals related to your son’s emergency counseling appointments).

However, your form is currently marked INCOMPLETE because the "Position ID" field was left blank. This must match the identifier used by the County (format typically "FM-####"). In your narrative you referenced having previously been in positions FM-3184 and FM-4021, and you attached a 2023 performance review that lists FM-4307, but none of those appear to be your current assignment.

Please locate your most recent assignment or reclassification letter from Facilities Management HR. The position ID is usually in the upper right corner near your name and employee number. You may also find it on:

• Your latest performance evaluation form
• A copy of your job posting for "Building Maintenance Engineer II – Courts"
• Any memo documenting your shift/assignment change when you were moved from Leighton to a rotating courts/hospitals schedule

Once you have the correct position ID, reply to this email with the FM number so we can finalize your intake and assign a staff representative.

In solidarity,

AFSCME Council 31 Intake Team

metadata.subject: Incomplete Grievance Intake – Missing Position ID

artifacts.content:
From: Marcus Ellison <marcus.ellison83@gmail.com>
To: DFM Labor Relations <laborrelations@cookcountyil.gov>
Date: 2026-02-21 08:17
Subject: Clarification on Supervisor Listing for Pre-Disciplinary Hearing

Good morning,

I’m working through the packet you sent for my pre-disciplinary hearing and had a question about the “immediate supervisor” section on the employee and department information form.

For most of 2024 when I was bouncing between Stroger and the Leighton Courthouse, my day-to-day direction came from different people. At Stroger, when I got the tardiness write-up tied to the January 23, 2025 incident with my probation check-in at Markham Courthouse, I was reporting directly to **Shift Engineer Robert McCann** on the 7:00 a.m.–3:00 p.m. shift. Later, when I transferred back to the courthouse, the work orders and PMs were usually assigned by **Chief Engineer Harold “Hal” Peterson**, even though he technically oversaw multiple buildings.

The form just says “immediate supervisor,” but my discipline history spans time under both McCann and Peterson. For example, when I skipped the full PM checklist on the 5th floor air handler on 2024-11-04 so I could leave early for that public defender meeting about Jalen’s truancy case, Hal was the one who signed off on the corrective memo.

For accuracy, should I list McCann for the Stroger-related items and Peterson for the courthouse-related items, or only the person who signed the most recent evaluation? I don’t want the form kicked back for being incomplete.

Thank you,

Marcus D. Ellison
Building Maintenance Engineer II (on paid administrative leave)
Cook County Department of Facilities Management

metadata.subject: Clarification on Supervisor Listing for Pre-Disciplinary Hearing

artifacts.content:
From: Tiffany Ellison <tiffany.ellison79@gmail.com>
To: Marcus Ellison <marcus.ellison83@gmail.com>
Date: 2026-02-10 19:42
Subject: Union stuff & who you should put down as your boss

Marc,

I read through that draft statement you texted me. When they ask for “immediate supervisor” on those County forms, don’t overthink it. Back when I had that write-up at the clinic, HR told me to list the person who actually did my evaluations, not whoever yelled at me on the floor.

Didn’t you say your evaluations at Stroger were done by **Assistant Chief Engineer Carla Donnelly**? Even if the shift engineer or control room guys were the ones paging you about hot/cold calls, HR just wants a name they recognize. I remember you saying Donnelly was the one who sat you down after March 6, 2025, when you took that County vehicle over to my place at 79th and Cottage Grove to calm things down with me and my idiot ex instead of handling that 8th floor complaint.

Same thing with the new mess: for the 2026-01-29 chemical spill in Mechanical Room 3B, just put whoever signed the last performance review, and then explain the rest in your own words. You already admitted to me that you stayed in there coughing and lightheaded trying to clean up the biocide because you were scared of another suspension and already two months behind on rent and child support.

Anyway, call your steward again before you submit anything. Don’t let them twist your words.

Love,
Tiff

metadata.subject: Union stuff & who you should put down as your boss

artifacts.content:
Title: Drafting Statement – Supervisor & Discipline History
Date: 2026-02-18 18:30–20:30
Location: 4832 W Cermak Rd, Apt 3B
Attendees: Marcus Ellison

Description:

Block time to work on written statement for Cook County Bureau of Human Resources.

To cover during this session:

1. EMPLOYEE & DEPARTMENT INFO SECTION
   - Double-check job title (Building Maintenance Engineer II).
   - For “immediate supervisor,” decide whether to list **Chief Engineer Harold Peterson** or **Assistant Chief Engineer Carla Donnelly** for the period covering the January 29, 2026 incident in Mechanical Room 3B.
   - Note that on the Stroger side, day-to-day direction often came from **Shift Engineer Robert McCann**, but performance reviews were not signed by him.

2. BASIS FOR RECOMMENDED DISCIPLINARY ACTION
   - Explain decision on 2026-01-29 to stay in Mechanical Room 3B and attempt to clean up the biocide spill while coughing and feeling lightheaded instead of calling it in, motivated by fear of another suspension while already two months behind on rent at 4832 W Cermak Rd, Apt 3B and paying $450/month child support to Danielle per the October 2023 order.
   - Explain February 5, 2026 interview at BHR where I said I followed lockout/tagout and spill procedures even though I hadn’t, and how exhaustion from overnight care for Mom at Loyola dialysis three times a week since November 2025 contributed to poor judgment.

3. PRIOR DISCIPLINE
   - Reference 2024 tardiness, 2024-11-04 skipped PM checklist, 2025-01-23 late arrival tied to probation officer check-in.

From: BHR Intake <bhr-intake@cookcountyil.gov>
To: Marcus Ellison <marcus.ellison83@gmail.com>
Date: 2026-02-07 15:09
Subject: Incomplete Employee Information – Please Update Supervisor Field

Mr. Ellison,

During our review of your Pre-Disciplinary Meeting Response Form, we noted that the "employee and department information > immediate supervisor" field was left blank.

For purposes of the record, please provide the name of the individual who functioned as your immediate supervisor at the time of the events described in your statement. This is typically the person responsible for conducting your performance evaluations and approving leave. For example, if your annual evaluations were signed by **Assistant Chief Engineer Carla Donnelly**, you would list Ms. Donnelly in that field, even if your daily assignments were given by a shift engineer.

Please also confirm that the narrative you provided regarding the January 29, 2026 incident in Mechanical Room 3B is complete. You referenced remaining in the room to attempt to clean up a biocide spill while experiencing coughing and lightheadedness. You may, if you wish, elaborate on any mitigating circumstances, including financial stressors (e.g., rent arrears at your current address or child support obligations) that influenced your decision-making.

Finally, your file reflects prior discipline, including the November 4, 2024 failure to complete a full preventive maintenance checklist and the January 23, 2025 late arrival associated with your probation officer check-in at the Markham Courthouse. If you believe your current caregiver responsibilities for your mother’s dialysis treatments at Loyola University Medical Center impacted your performance, you may address that as well.

Please reply by 2026-02-12.

Sincerely,

Angela Ruiz
HR Generalist
Cook County Bureau of Human Resources

metadata.subject: Incomplete Employee Information – Please Update Supervisor Field

artifacts.content:

Date: 2026-02-13 11:00–11:45
Location: Phone – from car outside Loyola Dialysis
Attendees: Marcus Ellison, Union Steward (Local 399)

Description:

Agenda for call with union steward about pre-disciplinary paperwork and supervisor info.

1. Immediate Supervisor Question
   - Ask whether to list **Chief Engineer Harold Peterson** or **Assistant Chief Engineer Carla Donnelly** as "immediate supervisor" for the January 29, 2026 Mechanical Room 3B incident.
   - Clarify that day-to-day direction at Leighton Courthouse often came from the control room operators and shift engineer, but performance reviews and leave approvals went through Peterson’s office until Donnelly took over some responsibilities.

2. Basis for Recommended Termination

   - Discuss February 5, 2026 interview where I told them I followed lockout/tagout and chemical spill procedures even though I hadn’t, and how overnight trips to Loyola University Medical Center for Mom’s dialysis since November 2025 left me exhausted.

3. Prior Incidents
   - Review March 6, 2025 incident leaving Stroger in County vehicle #DMF-214 for Tiffany’s domestic dispute.
   - Review January 23, 2025 late arrival due to 6:15 a.m. probation officer check-in at Markham.

metadata.title: Call w/ Union Steward re: Listing Supervisor
metadata.location: Phone – from car outside Loyola Dialysis

artifacts.content:
From: Marcus D. Ellison <m.ellison@cookcountyil.gov>
To: Thomas J. Kelleher <tkelleher@local399.org>
Date: 2026-02-18 08:42 AM
Subject: Clarifying supervisor info for grievance paperwork

Tom,

I’m filling out the union grievance intake form you sent over and I’m stuck on the “immediate supervisor” line. The HR packet from when I first got assigned to the Leighton Criminal Courthouse still lists **Mark Ruiz** as my direct supervisor from back when I transferred over in March 2019, but the day‑to‑day reality has been different the last couple years.

For day shift coverage, I usually report issues to whoever’s handling the courthouse facilities desk. For example, on 2026-01-29 when I ignored the posted lockout/tagout steps in Mechanical Room 3B and tried to clear that recirculation pump while it was still energized, I was more worried about Mark thinking I was slacking again because of the final warning from my three‑day suspension on 2025-05-12 for taking a County van to Danielle’s apartment on W. 63rd to drop off Jalen and Ariana.

On the newer HR forms, sometimes they list **“Courthouse Facilities Supervisor – Day Shift”** instead of a name, which just confuses things more. Before I submit anything to Labor Relations, I want to make sure I’m using the title they expect so they don’t kick the packet back.

Can you confirm whether I should write “Mark Ruiz – Courthouse Facilities Supervisor” on the immediate supervisor line, or if the County wants some generic title there instead of a specific person’s name?

Thanks,
Marcus
Building Maintenance Engineer II
Cook County Dept. of Facilities Management

metadata.subject: Clarifying supervisor info for grievance paperwork

artifacts.content:
Title: Prep call w/ union re: supervisor chain & incidents
Date: 2026-03-03 03:30 PM – 04:30 PM
Location: Phone – Marcus cell
Attendees: Marcus Ellison; Thomas Kelleher (Local 399)

Description:

Agenda for call:

1. Go over who counts as my “immediate supervisor” for different locations:
   - George N. Leighton Criminal Courthouse – historically listed as **Mark Ruiz (Courthouse Facilities Supervisor)** on my assignment sheet.
   - Stroger Hospital boiler room – radio traffic usually goes through the **Control Center Lead Engineer** on duty.
   - Juvenile Temporary Detention Center – evening shift direction mostly from the **JTDC Facilities Shift Supervisor**.

2. Review how those reporting lines factor into discipline:
   - 10/02/2025: Left the 5th floor AHU PM checklist half‑complete at Leighton after taking a call about my mom, Loretta, being admitted to ICU at Mount Sinai with complications from diabetes and congestive heart failure; never got back to finish the task.
   - 12/03/2025: Diverted County pool vehicle #DMF-214 about 40 minutes to check on my dad Darryl at Fresenius Kidney Care on W. 47th; missed two radio checks while assigned to the Stroger boiler room.

3. Figure out what title to write on the “immediate supervisor” line for the new County disciplinary response form (they don’t make it clear if they want a name like **“Mark Ruiz”** or just a generic title like “Facilities Supervisor – Day Shift”).

4. Draft bullet points for my statement before the arbitration prep meeting.

metadata.title: Prep call w/ union re: supervisor chain & incidents

artifacts.content:
From: HR Records <hr_records@cookcountyil.gov>
To: Marcus D. Ellison <m.ellison@cookcountyil.gov>
Date: 2026-02-05 10:11 AM
Subject: Request to confirm supervisor for personnel file audit

Mr. Ellison,

As part of the Department of Facilities Management personnel file audit, we are asking employees to verify the supervisor information currently on record.

Our database for your position (Building Maintenance Engineer II, Courthouse/Medical Campus rotation) shows the following historical entries:

2019–2022
Immediate Supervisor: **Mark Ruiz – Courthouse Facilities Supervisor (Day Shift)**

2022–Present
Immediate Supervisor: **Courthouse Facilities Supervisor – Day Shift (title only)**

Because there was a change to a title‑only listing, we need you to confirm the appropriate name and title for the “immediate supervisor” field as of January 29, 2026 (the date referenced in the pending disciplinary matter involving Mechanical Room 3B at the George N. Leighton Criminal Courthouse).

Please reply to this email with:
1. The name you consider your immediate supervisor for the courthouse assignment as of 2026-01-29.
2. The correct title (e.g., “Courthouse Facilities Supervisor – Day Shift”).

This information will be used only to update your personnel file and will not change the substance of any pending discipline related to the incident in which you attempted to clear a recirculation pump while energized and did not immediately report the resulting chemical spill.

If you have questions about chain of command or reporting structure, you may copy your union representative on your response.

Sincerely,

Angela Price
HR Analyst
Cook County Department of Facilities Management

metadata.subject: Request to confirm supervisor for personnel file audit

artifacts.content:
From: Marcus D. Ellison <m.ellison@cookcountyil.gov>
To: Angela Price <hr_records@cookcountyil.gov>
CC: Thomas Kelleher <tkelleher@local399.org>
Date: 2026-02-06 07:58 AM
Subject: Re: Request to confirm supervisor for personnel file audit

Angela,

Following up on your request to verify my immediate supervisor information.

For the courthouse side, my understanding has always been that **Mark Ruiz** is the person I answer to on day shift. Even when the forms switched over to listing just “Courthouse Facilities Supervisor – Day Shift,” everyone on our crew still referred to Mark as our boss. When I got the three‑day suspension on 2025-05-12 for taking the County van to Danielle’s place on W. 63rd during my shift to drop off Jalen and Ariana, it was Mark who signed off as the recommending supervisor.

Same thing with the more recent issues. On the morning of February 9, 2026, when I left my assigned post for about 55 minutes and took County vehicle #DMF-214 over to Danielle’s apartment on S. Indiana to check on Jalen after his panic attack from the ADHD meds adjustment at Lurie Children’s, I knew that was going to go through Mark’s office once the missed radio calls about the Stroger surgical wing temperature alarm got flagged.

So, for your audit as of 01/29/2026, please list:

Immediate Supervisor: **Mark Ruiz**
Title: Courthouse Facilities Supervisor – Day Shift

Let me know if you need anything else.

Marcus
Building Maintenance Engineer II

metadata.subject: Re: Request to confirm supervisor for personnel file audit

artifacts.content:
Title: DMF Investigatory Meeting – Chain of Command & Attendance Issues
Date: 2026-02-24 09:00 AM – 10:30 AM
Location: DMF Conference Room 4B, 118 N. Clark St.
Attendees: Marcus Ellison; Mark Ruiz; Angela Price (HR); Union Rep (Local 399)

Description:

Purpose: Pre‑disciplinary investigatory meeting to clarify:
- Who Marcus reports to as his immediate supervisor at each assigned facility.
- How that chain of command relates to recent incidents.

Talking points Marcus should prepare:
1. Courthouse assignment:
   - Explain that he has always treated **Mark Ruiz** as his direct boss on courthouse day shift, even when HR forms only listed “Courthouse Facilities Supervisor – Day Shift.”
   - Walk through the January 29, 2026 Mechanical Room 3B event where he ignored lockout/tagout and tried to clear the recirculation pump energized, knocked over the chemical shelf, and then stayed in the room to mop up instead of calling it in because he was already on a final warning.

2. Other locations and supervisors:
   - Stroger Hospital boiler room: clarify that instructions usually come from the **Control Center Lead Engineer**, which is why missing the radio calls during the February 9, 2026 temperature alarm in the surgical wing is being tied back to that chain of command.
   - Juvenile Temporary Detention Center: describe who was acting as the **JTDC Facilities Shift Supervisor** on October 14, 2025, when Marcus left rounds for about 50 minutes to drop off a $600 cash child support payment at 4832 W. Cermak Rd. and missed three radio calls.

3. Ask HR to specify exactly which supervisor name/title they want on the “immediate supervisor” line for future forms.
